author: cursor-agent

patch.value: Coordinate strict-vs-auto history-stream handling and restore recovery hardening across commands, tests, and docs.
History stream resilience and restore recovery hardening
Implement settings-driven missing-history stream policy and restore recovery for missing/deleted item files while preserving default compatibility.

Default mode auto-creates missing history streams safely, strict mode fails deterministically across history-touching commands, restore can recover missing/deleted item files from history, and full verification/docs updates pass.
No active duplicate tracking item exists; target command/test/doc surfaces are identified.
Prevents silent drift and improves recoverability
More reliable history and restore workflows for maintainers and agents
Deterministic missing-stream policy and recovery behavior
Current behavior is inconsistent across commands and recovery scenarios
Improves reliability for all users when history streams/items are missing

value.author: cursor-agent
value.text: Created after de-dup checks found no active item for strict missing-history policy plus restore missing-item recovery scope.

value.author: cursor-agent
value.text: Implementation follows docs-first updates plus command/test hardening with compatibility-safe defaults.

message: Create epic for history stream resilience and restore recovery | explicit_unset=learnings,assignee,parent,reviewer,blocked_by,blocked_reason,unblock_note,reporter,severity,environment,repro_steps,resolution,expected_result,actual_result,affected_version,fixed_version,regression

value.author: cursor-agent
value.text: Epic delivery evidence: implemented missing-stream policy configuration and enforcement across history-touching paths, added restore recovery for missing/deleted item files from history-only state, updated docs/changelog, and verified with full build/test sweeps. Known residual: global coverage gate remains below strict 100% thresholds (97.32/95.02/98.10/97.02).
